description_long: This bottle is crafted in a stylized, anthropomorphic form, resembling a seated figure with a rounded body and a sculpted head. The surface is matte and unglazed, with a uniform earthy terracotta color and visible hand-molded details, such as facial features and arms resting on the body. The front displays two small, dark eye holes and a protruding nose, while the arms are attached to the sides, one bent at the elbow. The back features a horizontal slot opening, possibly for filling or pouring, and the overall shape is slightly asymmetrical, with subtle surface cracks and texture variations. The bottle appears to be hollow, as indicated by the slot and the thinness of the material at the opening. There are no visible labels, branding, or decorative patterns, and the object has a primitive, handmade aesthetic.
description: A matte, terracotta bottle shaped like a seated figure with sculpted facial features and a slot opening on the back. The surface is earthy, textured, and unadorned, giving it a handmade appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the anthropomorphic face with two eye holes, a nose, and both arms visible, one bent at the elbow.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the protruding nose, the arm bent forward, and the rounded body profile.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals a horizontal slot opening at the back of the head and the smooth, unadorned back surface.
description_view_3: Right side view shows the other arm and the continuous curvature of the body, with the head and facial profile visible.
